Package nonprofitbookkeeping.model

Class ChartOfAccounts

java.lang.Object
nonprofitbookkeeping.model.ChartOfAccounts
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ChartOfAccounts extends Object implements Serializable
Mutable container that holds every Account in the company and supports parent/child relationships.
See Also:

    Method
    Description
    void

    Adds a top-level (root) account to the chart of accounts.

    Adds a child account under the specified parent account.

    Builds a lookup table of account number to Account.

    Returns a list of accounts that are direct children of the specified parent account.

    Returns a list of every account that does not have a parent account.

    Removes an account (and its descendants) from the chart of accounts using its account number.

    Removes the specified target account and all its descendants (if any) from the chart.

  • Constructor Details

    • ChartOfAccounts

      public ChartOfAccounts()

  • Method Details

    • getRootAccounts

      public List<Account> getRootAccounts()
      Returns a list of every account that does not have a parent account. These are considered the top-level accounts in the chart.
      Returns:
      A new List<Account> containing all root accounts. Returns an empty list if there are no root accounts or no accounts at all.

    • getChildren

      public List<Account> getChildren(Account parent)
      Returns a list of accounts that are direct children of the specified parent account.
      Parameters:
      parent - The Account whose children are to be retrieved.
      Returns:
      A new List<Account> containing the direct children of the parent. Returns an empty list if the parent has no children or the parent is not in the chart.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if parent is null.

    • addAccount

      public void addAccount(Account root)
      Adds a top-level (root) account to the chart of accounts. The account's parent will be explicitly set to null.
      Parameters:
      root - The Account to add as a root account.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if root is null.

    • addSubAccount

      public void addSubAccount(Account parent, Account child)
      Adds a child account under the specified parent account.
      Parameters:
      parent - The Account that will be the parent of the child.
      child - The Account to add as a child.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if parent or child is null.

    • removeAccount

      public void removeAccount(Account target)
      Removes the specified target account and all its descendants (if any) from the chart. If the target account is null, the method does nothing.
      Parameters:
      target - The Account to remove.

    • getAccountNames

      public String getAccountNames()
      Returns a comma-separated string of all account names in the chart. This is a convenience method for a quick overview of account names.
      Returns:
      A string containing all account names, separated by commas. Returns an empty string if there are no accounts.

    • getAccountByName

      public Account getAccountByName(String accountName)
      Retrieves an account by its display name. This is a convenience lookup used when UI components store the account name instead of the unique account number.
      Parameters:
      accountName - the name of the account
      Returns:
      the matching Account or null if none found

    • getAccount

      public Account getAccount(String accountNumber)
      Retrieves a specific Account from the chart by its account number. This method provides a convenient way to look up an account using its unique identifier. It utilizes an internally generated map for efficient retrieval.
      Parameters:
      accountNumber - The account number of the account to retrieve. If null or blank, this method will return null.
      Returns:
      The Account object if found, or null if no account matches the given account number, or if the input accountNumber is invalid.

    • createAccountNumberMap

      public AccountNumberMap createAccountNumberMap()
      Builds a lookup table of account number to Account.
      Returns:
      a new AccountNumberMap for the current accounts

    • getAccounts

      public List<Account> getAccounts()
      Returns a list of all accounts contained in this chart of accounts. This list includes all accounts, whether they are root accounts or child accounts.

      The returned list is a shallow copy of the internal list of accounts. Modifications to the returned list will not affect the chart of accounts itself. However, modifications to the Account objects within the list will affect the accounts stored in the chart.

      Returns:
      A new List<Account> containing all accounts in the chart. Returns an empty list if the chart of accounts has no accounts.

    • replaceAllAccounts

      public void replaceAllAccounts(List<Account> accounts)
      Replaces the current accounts with the provided collection. Existing entries are removed first.
      Parameters:
      accounts - accounts to install, may be null

    • removeAccount

      public boolean removeAccount(String accountNumber)
      Removes an account (and its descendants) from the chart of accounts using its account number.
      Parameters:
      accountNumber - The account number of the account to remove.
      Returns:
      true if the account was found (and thus removal was attempted), false if no account with the given number was found (in which case no action is taken). Note: The current implementation always returns true after attempting removal, it might be more accurate to reflect if the account existed and was removed.
